# Developer-Experience Checklist: PRSG-010 Harden the Hatch + O5 Monster Epics

**Purpose**: Validate that PRSG-010 operator-facing requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable before implementation, with emphasis on blocked-run re-slicing guidance, O5-vs-normal-split guidance, and explicit exception education after boilerplate removal.
**Created**: 2026-06-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md) | [data-model.md](../data-model.md) | [contracts/](../contracts/)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

**Depth**: Formal release gate
**Audience**: Operators, spec authors, and PR reviewers
**Focus**: Concrete re-slicing next steps, O5 monster-epic decision guidance, and non-boilerplate explicit exception documentation.

## Blocked Operator Re-Slicing Guidance

- [ ] CHK001 Are blocked-run requirements explicit that final gate blocks appear as actionable re-slicing states rather than advisory warnings? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK002 Does the re-slicing packet require enough PRSG-007, PRSG-008, and PRSG-009 context for a blocked operator to resume without reconstructing workflow state from prose? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK003 Are operator-visible re-slicing next steps specified as an ordered, concrete command/path sequence rather than a prose-only `next_action` string? [Resolved - Spec §FR-006/§FR-008, data-model.md, and reslicing-packet.schema.json now require `operator_steps` with concrete phase, command, paths, use condition, and unblock condition]
- [ ] CHK004 Does blocked-run guidance define how the operator chooses between rerunning PRSG-007 routing, regenerating the PRSG-008 layer plan, or handing off to PRSG-009 multi-PR emission based on packet evidence? [Resolved - data-model.md now ties `resume.resume_from` to packet evidence for PRSG-007 reroute, PRSG-008 regeneration, or PRSG-009 handoff]
- [ ] CHK005 Is the under-5-minute re-slicing outcome objectively tied to status output and packet fields? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK006 Are no-PR side effects and blocked PR-preparation operations visible enough for operators to trust that a blocked run did not partially emit a PR? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]

## O5 Versus Normal Split-PR Guidance

- [ ] CHK007 Are O5 requirements clear that O5 is reserved for genuine monster epics and is not a replacement for the normal PRSG-007/008/009 split path? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK008 Does scaffold guidance require explaining that normal split-PR remains sufficient when ordinary routing and layer planning can produce reviewable slices? [Resolved - Spec §FR-010 now makes PRSG-007/008/009 split-PR the default, with O5 only when ordinary routing/layer planning cannot produce reviewable slices]
- [ ] CHK009 Are O5 trigger and non-trigger conditions measurable enough that scaffold/status guidance can avoid nudging ordinary oversized work into a monster-epic parent? [Resolved - Spec §Assumptions and §SC-007 now make oversized one-PR estimates insufficient for O5 when normal split-PR can slice thin enough]
- [ ] CHK010 Are O5 child topology requirements consistent with developer expectations for flat sibling specs, curated MOC links, and no nested child directories? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012 §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK011 Are O5 validation diagnostics required to be actionable enough for spec authors to fix topology problems without reverse-engineering the parent manifest schema? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK012 Does the plan assign O5 scaffold/status guidance to a specific slice with parity and deterministic fixture coverage? [Completeness, Plan §Split Plan]

## Explicit Exception Guidance Without Boilerplate

- [ ] CHK013 Are generated roadmap, workflow, template, and PR-description artifacts prohibited from carrying live copy-pasteable exception override lines? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004 §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK014 Does the spec require non-copy-pasteable operator guidance that still explains when and how a valid explicit typed exception is truly allowed? [Resolved - Spec §FR-004 and §SC-008 now require prose/table/placeholder exception education that names classes and provenance without standalone valid pragma lines]
- [ ] CHK015 Are valid exception requirements clear enough for operators to distinguish a branch-added CONTRACT Markdown exception from generated, mutable, code-fence, PR-description, or commit-message text? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003 §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK016 Does the review packet requirement preserve exception class, provenance, and gate result for reviewer understanding when an exception is honored? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK017 Does the plan explicitly include docs/template/parity work so exception education changes stay aligned across Claude and Codex surfaces? [Completeness, Plan §Split Plan]

## Cross-Surface Developer Experience

- [ ] CHK018 Are scaffold, status, autopilot, and Codex mirror surfaces named where operator-facing wording must change? [Traceability, Plan §Declared File Operations]
- [ ] CHK019 Are measurable success criteria defined for all three developer-experience outcomes: re-slicing guidance, O5-vs-normal-split guidance, and exception education after boilerplate removal? [Resolved - Spec §SC-004, §SC-007, and §SC-008 now cover concrete re-slicing actions, O5 default-boundary guidance, and non-boilerplate exception education]
- [ ] CHK020 Are default validations listed for each operator-facing surface so reviewers know how to verify wording, parity, and deterministic behavior changes? [Completeness, Plan §Testing]
- [ ] CHK021 Are non-goals clear enough to prevent developer-experience work from expanding into nested O5 scanning, PRSG-009 redesign, or disabling typed exceptions? [Consistency, Plan §PR Review Packet Source]

## Verification Pass

- [ ] CHK022 Does the remediated re-slicing packet contract require concrete `operator_steps` with phase, command, required paths, use condition, and unblock condition? [Completeness, Contract reslicing-packet.schema.json]
- [ ] CHK023 Does the remediated data model explain how packet evidence selects PRSG-007 reroute, PRSG-008 layer-plan regeneration, or PRSG-009 handoff? [Clarity, Data Model §Re-slicing Packet]
- [ ] CHK024 Does the remediated scaffold guidance requirement make normal PRSG-007/008/009 split-PR the default before O5 is considered? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK025 Do the remediated success criteria separately measure concrete re-slicing guidance, O5 decision guidance, and non-boilerplate exception education? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004 §SC-007 §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK026 Does the remediated exception education requirement preserve operator understanding of explicit exceptions without emitting a standalone valid pragma line? [Coverage, Spec §FR-004 §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK027 Does the remediated split plan require verification for `operator_steps`, O5 default-boundary guidance, and generated exception-education negative checks? [Completeness, Plan §Split Plan]

## Notes

- Gap markers identify missing or under-specified requirements and contracts, not implementation failures.
